notes: Reproduced on Android emulator (API 36, arm64) with SkiaSharp 3.116.1, 3.119.2 (latest stable), and main branch source. When an SKGLSurfaceView is used with PreserveEGLContextOnPause=false and the app transitions background to foreground, the GL rendering thread crashes with SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference) immediately after OnResume. The EGL context is destroyed on pause and recreated on resume, but SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated is empty and does not reset the GRContext, causing the stale GRContext to dereference a null pointer. The bug persists on the main branch — no fix has been applied.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! We've confirmed the issue. The SKGLSurfaceView crashes with a native SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference) when returning from the background.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| SkiaSharp 3.116.1 | ❌ Reproduced — SIGSEGV in GLThread |
| SkiaSharp 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ Reproduced — SIGSEGV in GLThread |
| main (source) | ❌ Reproduced — SIGSEGV in GLThread |

**Reproduction:** When `PreserveEGLContextOnPause` is `false` (or when Android destroys the EGL context), the GL rendering thread crashes with `Fatal signal 11 (SIGSEGV), fault addr 0x0` immediately after the activity resumes. No frames are rendered after the resume.

**Workarounds:**
1. Switch to `SKCanvasView` (CPU-based rendering) if GPU acceleration isn't critical — this avoids the EGL lifecycle issue entirely.
2. If you must use GL rendering, ensure `PreserveEGLContextOnPause = true` is set (though this is not guaranteed to work on all devices/API levels).

We're tracking this for a fix.

actionability.reason: Bug reproduces on all tested versions including main branch. The root cause is well-understood from triage (empty OnSurfaceCreated vs TextureView FreeContext call), but needs a code fix.

workarounds: Switch to SKCanvasView (CPU-based rendering) which does not depend on the EGL context lifecycle
Set PreserveEGLContextOnPause = true on the SKGLSurfaceView (may not work on all devices)
